The prohibitionist's candidate for
president has one thing which all
the other presidential candidates
may well envy him-the love of
scores of men whom he has res
cued from drunkards' graves. W.


you see the suit have one?
Well, 1t was made for me by the
man whom I literally picked from
the gutter where he had been left
bya.hose who believed it the only
fit place for him"


Thus Eugene w. Chain. And he
will go on to tell how the man is
now the proprietor of tailoring
business yielding an income OF
$4,000 a year. And he could, II
disposed. tell of many similar
cases.


Seven years ago Chain left a
comfortable law practice in Wis
.consin to go to Chicago and take
charge of the Washingtonian home
at salary of $500. Since then
ahe has come into personal con-
tact with 5000 drunkards of the
extreme type. In large proper
ton of cases he has been able to
hip these men to take a new hold
on life.


A big, strong. good natured man
is this prohibition candidate. He
weighs 1S5 pounds. can turn a
handspring at So as well as he did
at 16, and boasts that he has never
felt a pain or experienced sick
day In his life. This Is the herit-
age of boyhood on a farm near
Milwaukee.


Last year Chain made sos
speeches. He spoke in eight differ
ent state, and did not miss meet
ing or arrive late for one. He did
not miss train nor experience a
hitch in his arrangement III all
that year.


Campaigning is no new stunt to
Chain. For 30 years he has been
on the lecture platform. and in his
home state of Illinois he is well
nor and for judge of the circuit
court. His lecture on Lincoln the
Man of Sorrows" is circulated as a
campaign document by the pro-
hibition party. For the past IL
year he has devoted his time to
the prohibition cause


Chain is a Methodist His father
died when he was II, leaving 10
children. he being the oldest boy
at home. Thus 1t fell to his lot to
run the farm for his mother and
the eight younger ones, and this he
did until he came of age. While
working on the farm he carried
on his school work. and saved to
go to college. and finally was sue
cessful in working his way through
the state university. He was a
.classmate there of Robert NJ. La
Toilette. of whom he is a strong
admirer


Chain writes with confidence
about the outcome of the election
In November.


1--WE want no prohibitionists to
say 1t is not possible ford us to
elect our ticket this year." he said
in his message to this newspaper
THAT Is equivalent to saying 1t Is
impossible for majority of our
